_comment_provider: One of: Ollama, Anthropic, OpenAI, AzureOpenAI, Custom
_comment_apikey: DPAPI-encrypted at rest after first save. Do NOT paste a real key here -- run option 99 (AI Assistant) inside the tool to configure interactively, or copy this file to ai_config.json and replace REPLACE_ME.
_comment_portable: ai_config.json is encrypted per-user and is NOT portable across machines or accounts.

Privacy._comment_ExternalRedaction: Enabled | Disabled. When Enabled, UPNs / emails / GUIDs / tenant IDs / display names captured from cmdlet args are tokenized (<UPN_1>, <GUID_3>, ...) before being sent to non-local providers, and restored on the AI's response. Secrets (JWTs, sk-... keys, cert thumbprints) are ALWAYS tokenized regardless of this setting and regardless of provider.
Privacy._comment_RedactInAuditLog: Enabled | Disabled. Disabled (default) writes raw cmdlet text + results to the audit log for forensics. Enabled writes the tokenized form. Either way, -Password / -Credential / -AccessToken / -ClientSecret / -ApiKey / -Secret / -Token values are always redacted in the audit log (commit 5 behavior).
Privacy._comment_ExternalPayloadCapBytes: 0 = no cap. Otherwise the outbound message content is truncated AFTER redaction with an explicit [TRUNCATED N BYTES] marker, so the operator sees that context was cut. Recommended: 8192. Applies only to external providers.
Privacy._comment_TrustedProviders: Lowercase provider names to treat like localhost (no PII redaction; secrets still scrubbed). Example: ["azure-openai"] if your Azure OpenAI deployment sits in the same compliance boundary as the M365 tenant you administer. WARNING: only add a provider here after reviewing its data-handling terms. Provider retention defaults: Anthropic enterprise = zero retention; OpenAI = 30-day abuse-monitoring retention unless your org has opted out; Azure OpenAI = governed by your Azure subscription.

Notifications._comment_email: DefaultEmailFrom is the UPN /me/sendMail will spoof when set; leave empty to use the operator's mailbox. SecurityTeamRecipients / OperationsTeamRecipients route Critical / Warning / Info severities respectively (see Notifications.ps1 dispatcher).
Notifications._comment_webhooks: Teams webhook URLs are sensitive (anyone with the URL can post). Stored DPAPI-encrypted on save (DPAPI:... prefix). Set via /privacy menu or Start-NotificationsSetup -- never paste raw URLs into this file in source control.
Notifications._comment_dryrun: Set DryRunNotifications=true to log each send to the audit log without actually firing. Useful for testing health checks.
